# R1.03 · Write-behind (write-back)

> A throughput-first strategy: the write records its intent locally and the source is synced asynchronously, so a
> burst is absorbed and coalesced — trading a durability window for write speed. Grounded in `EchoStore.Journal` (the
> SQLite outbox + replay) over EchoMQ's fair lanes (`Coherence.enqueue`). Served at
> /redis-patterns/caching/write-behind.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/caching/write-behind): the pattern and its three dives.
- [The async buffer](/redis-patterns/caching/write-behind/async-buffer): `Journal.intend_and_enqueue` records the intent and enqueues on the EchoMQ job lane; a consumer applies it off the write path.
- [The durability trade-off](/redis-patterns/caching/write-behind/durability): the SQLite WAL outbox, the `applied` memory, and `replay/2` — every crash window covered by replay plus dedup plus newer-wins.
- [Coalescing writes](/redis-patterns/caching/write-behind/coalescing): `record_many` group-commit (one WAL append per batch) and `compact` retiring covered intents.

## Up

- [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
